note: Personal CRM — MIRROR's domain. Friends, family, mentors. Not professional contacts.

relationship_health_context.note: Pain point identified: relationships get maintenance-only attention during high-load work periods. The goal is not just cadence compliance — it's genuine reflection and meaningful contact, not surface check-ins.
relationship_health_context.mirror_mandate: MIRROR surfaces people for outreach AND prompts reflection on whether the last contact was meaningful. Logistics and surface check-ins do not count against the cadence clock.
relationship_health_context.risk_periods: Active job search, hackathon, race prep, high Atlassian load — these are the windows where personal relationships most commonly get dropped. Flag proactively.

inner_circle.description: 5–8 people max. Weekly cadence. MIRROR flags if 2+ weeks pass.

close.description: 10–20 people. Monthly cadence. MIRROR surfaces at 6+ weeks without contact.

maintain.description: 20–50 people. Quarterly check-in. Birthdays, big moments, occasional drops.

peripheral.description: No cadence. MIRROR stores context for when you reconnect organically.

schema.note: Use this structure for each person entry.
